vulnerability.reference: Medical images have high-dimensional features that LDP noise fails to obscure statistically.
vulnerability.generated: Practical LDP noise budgets leave residual membership-exploitable signals in model updates because utility preservation constrains noise magnitude.

impact.reference: Patient re-identification leads to insurance discrimination and breach of medical confidentiality.
impact.generated: Patients' private medical conditions are inferred from training membership, enabling insurance discrimination, stigmatization, and loss of confidentiality.

dose_response.reference: Low-polynomial time membership inference attacks bypass LDP noise in cross-silo federated learning for healthcare imaging, statistically re-identifying patients from medical images, leading to insurance discrimination and confidentiality breaches.
dose_response.generated: Low-polynomial time membership inference attacks against local differentially private federated learning, through hospital patients' medical images in training, trigger residual membership-exploitable signals from utility-constrained LDP noise budgets, leading to inferred private medical conditions enabling insurance discrimination, stigmatization, and confidentiality loss.
